finding_results.evidence: 4 comparison instances of 'the way [X]' remain: (1-2) chapters/chapter_01.md:53 'the way the suit was, the way the commute was' — rhetorical double, costume/ritual vehicle; (3) chapters/chapter_01.md:135 'the way he knew the radiator's knock' — sensory/acoustic vehicle; (4) chapters/chapter_01.md:145 'the way you might feel if you'd rehearsed a scene' — theatrical vehicle. Proxy-office section (lines 49-105): 2 instances (line 53). Evening domestic section (lines 115-225): 2 instances (lines 135, 145). All vehicles belong to different metaphor families. Removed 10 instances across lines 3, 13, 19, 49, 103, 105, 115, 117, 123, 213 using varied replacement strategies: direct rendering (lines 3, 49, 117), sentence restructuring (lines 13, 19), comparison-frame deletion (lines 103, 105, 115), physical detail substitution (line 123), and character-specific reframing (line 213).
finding_results.notes: Line 53's double retained per rewrite direction as strongest candidate — rhetorical rather than habitual repetition. Non-comparison uses of 'the way' at lines 5 ('close all the way') and 47 ('the rest of the way') preserved as they are path/completeness descriptors, not simile structures.

finding_results.evidence: 5 combined instances of 'the specific/particular [noun]' remain: (1) chapters/chapter_01.md:31 'the particular quality of a room with one less body in it' — justified by following clause specifying what's particular; (2) chapters/chapter_01.md:33 'the particular late-February cold of New York — not dramatic, just persistent' — justified by following contrast; (3) chapters/chapter_01.md:87 'a particular dialect of English that Claude-state used for operational authorizations — syntactically correct, vocabularily familiar, and semantically opaque' — justified by following description; (4) chapters/chapter_01.md:107 'the specific pleasure of it: not the recognition, but the problem' — justified by following clause specifying what makes it specific; (5) chapters/chapter_01.md:225 'the specific emptiness of a container that had once held something and now held only the shape of what used to be there' — justified by extended image. Line 107 retains exactly 1 instance of 'specific' (down from 3 in original). Removed 7 instances at lines 71, 85, 105, 129, 139, 185, 223 via modifier deletion (lines 71, 85, 129), sensory detail substitution (lines 139, 223), and concrete noun replacement (lines 105, 185).
finding_results.notes: Each retained instance earns its modifier through a following clause or image that specifies what makes the thing specific or particular. The line 107 refrigeration-memory passage retains only 'the specific pleasure of it'; the matching instances at line 223 ('the specific weight,' 'the specific satisfaction') were reduced to bare nouns, which carry the precision through their context.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_01.md:225 — '*Shit.* Not aimed at anything — just the word, blunt and solitary in his head. Then it passed.' This profane interior thought occurs in Dan's couch-scene monologue (line 225, within the 139-225 range), where he is alone, drinking a beer, processing the divorce announcement. The word surfaces between the composed observations 'His marriage was ending. His job was a performance.' and 'The two facts lay next to each other on the couch with him' — a crack in the literary composure that the surrounding prose immediately smooths over, making the composure feel like effort rather than nature.
finding_results.notes: Placed in the couch scene rather than the divorce-announcement scene because Dan is alone — no audience for his composure, which makes the unguarded moment feel authentic rather than performed. 'Shit' chosen over 'fuck' as more reflexive and consistent with Dan's profanity profile ('common and unimaginative'). The profanity arrives 'with nothing attached' — no elaboration, no self-pity — then passes, and the composed observations resume. The contrast between the blunt syllable and the surrounding literary prose does the work the finding requested: one crack makes the composure feel like a coping mechanism, not a narrative default.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_01.md:1 — This finding references snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1 and requests regeneration of full-book findings with anchored references. The finding is a meta-level review-process issue (missing rewrite_direction in full_award review finding #21), not a chapter-content defect.
finding_results.notes: This finding cannot be addressed through chapter-level revision. It requires regeneration of the full-book award review (reviews/cycle_01/full_award.review.json) with complete finding fields, which is outside the scope of a single-chapter revision pass. Flagging as UNRESOLVED for the pipeline to handle at the appropriate review stage.

finding_results.evidence: Line 33: negation removed — 'The cold was the persistent late-February cold of New York, the kind that found the gaps in your coat and stayed there' (chapters/chapter_01.md:33). Line 85: retained as the single permitted instance — 'not sadness, not frustration, not even boredom exactly, but the hollow feeling of having performed a function he couldn't name' (chapters/chapter_01.md:85). Line 105: negation chain replaced with adverbs — 'invisibly, completely, the medium through which everything moved' (chapters/chapter_01.md:105). Line 225: negation chain replaced with physical image — 'a dull weight behind his breastbone, like something swallowed that hadn't gone all the way down' (chapters/chapter_01.md:225). The closing passage (line 225) now uses embodied simile while the post-meeting passage (line 85) retains the negation-chain — recognizably different sentence architectures.
finding_results.notes: Four instances of the negation-then-definition pattern existed in the current text (lines 33, 85, 105, 225). The finding cited three (33, 85, 225); line 105 also matched the pattern and was fixed to meet the acceptance test of 'at most one.' Line 85 retained as the strongest candidate per the finding's rewrite direction.

finding_results.evidence: The current chapter text contains no river/current metaphor at line 105 or line 123. Line 105 reads 'structure his hours — invisibly, completely, the medium through which everything moved' (chapters/chapter_01.md:105). Line 123 reads 'it had simply organized itself through repetition, each of them reaching for the same shelf until it stopped being a choice' (chapters/chapter_01.md:123). Neither passage contains a river or current metaphor.
finding_results.notes: The river/current metaphors cited in the finding ('the way a current structures a river' at line 105 and 'the way a river organizes its banks' at line 123) were present in the snapshot version but had already been removed from the current chapter text prior to this revision pass. No additional changes were needed.

finding_results.evidence: Seven passages restructured to break the trailing-clause default. Line 7: trailing appositive chain ('his father's old headphones looped around his neck like they always were, unplugged and purposeless, a comfort object he'd claimed...') replaced with three sentences using direct subject-verb construction (chapters/chapter_01.md:7). Line 53: trailing 'the way X was, the way Y was' replaced with dash-list and sentence break — 'the costume, the commute, the suit. You dressed for a job...' (chapters/chapter_01.md:53). Line 59: trailing relative clause chain on rubber plant split into three independent sentences (chapters/chapter_01.md:59). Line 67: character introductions restructured from 'Carolyn Park, who was...' relative clauses to direct 'Carolyn Park was...' subject-verb constructions (chapters/chapter_01.md:67). Line 71: 'a way of watching that involved' replaced with colon-introduced parallel structure — 'a technique: his eyes tracked the data while his mind floated' (chapters/chapter_01.md:71). Line 87: document ceremony passage broken from cascading relative clauses into short declaratives and fragments — 'Twelve pages of dense type. Dan recognized it...' (chapters/chapter_01.md:87). Line 197: 'which was always, which was the whole problem, because' cascade replaced with two short declarative sentences — 'They were always running well. That was the whole problem.' (chapters/chapter_01.md:197).
finding_results.notes: Five of six cited passages restructured (lines 7, 53, 59, 87, 197), plus two additional passages in the proxy-office section (lines 67, 71) to ensure no window of five consecutive paragraphs exceeds two trailing-clause instances. Line 13 (Mira description) left unchanged — its trailing clause is partially offset by the short declarative close ('They'd stopped sharing most things') and revising it would risk churn in a passage that works. The proxy-office section (lines 53-87) now has trailing-clause architecture in no more than 2 of any 5 consecutive paragraphs.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised chapter_01.md to address 3 of 4 findings (3 FIXED, 1 UNRESOLVED). The 'the way [X]' simile tic was reduced from 12+ instances to 4, distributed across sections with distinct metaphor vehicles and varied replacement strategies. The 'specific/particular' modifier tic was reduced from 12 instances to 5, with each retained instance earning its precision through a following clause. An unguarded profane thought ('*Shit.*') was inserted in the couch scene to crack Dan's literary composure at the chapter's emotional nadir. The fallback_full_award_contract finding is a review-process artifact that cannot be resolved through chapter editing. All revisions preserve heading contract, continuity, character voice profiles, dialogue contraction levels, and the aesthetic risk policy — no content was softened or sanitized.

All three findings resolved. The negation-then-definition pattern is reduced from four instances to one (line 85), with the closing passage (line 225) rewritten through embodied physical imagery rather than the negation chain, giving the chapter's two major reflective moments recognizably different sentence architectures. The river/current metaphor duplication was already absent from the current text. The trailing-clause monotony is broken across seven passages, with the proxy-office section (lines 53-87) restructured so that short declaratives, colon-introduced parallel structures, and sentence fragments now alternate with the remaining trailing-clause sentences. No content was softened, euphemized, or sanitized. Continuity details verified against the continuity sheet (Dan age 44, Theo age 9, F train commute, refrigerator 40-minute cycle, east-facing window, second-floor landing creak). Heading contract preserved. Dialogue register unchanged — all characters maintain their contraction levels and voice signatures.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=6, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=1).
